typecheck.details: BUILD SUCCEEDED (iOS). Also verified macOS build: BUILD SUCCEEDED.

lint.details: SwiftLint: warnings only (all pre-existing, zero errors). SwiftFormat: 0/471 files require formatting.

nonBlockingIssues.description: ToolType.wifiHeatmap reuses .cyan color already used by .vpnInfo, reducing visual distinction in tools grid.
Inconsistent accessibility identifier naming: some use 2-part (heatmap_emptyState) vs convention 3-part (heatmap_section_emptyState).
HeatmapProjectSummary.formattedDate creates new DateFormatter on every access. Static formatter would be more efficient.
HeatmapDashboardViewModel.loadProjects() performs synchronous filesystem I/O on MainActor.
FloorPlanImportViewModel downcasts wifiService to concrete WiFiInfoService for authorizationStatus, bypassing protocol abstraction and making location permission logic untestable with mocks.
PDF import runs synchronous CGContext rendering on MainActor. Large PDFs could cause UI jank.
FloorPlanCanvasView.clampOffset() does not actually clamp — image can be panned off-screen with no snap-back.
rasterizePDF() encodes CGImage to PNG Data then immediately decodes back to CGImage. Unnecessary encode-decode cycle doubles memory for PDF imports.
MeasurementMarker uses deprecated UIScreen.main.bounds for layout calculations. Should use GeometryReader container size.
RSSI-to-Color threshold logic duplicated identically in FloatingRSSIHUD.swift and MeasurementDetailSheet.swift.
FloatingRSSIHUD.signalIconName has redundant branches: rssi >= -50 and rssi >= -65 both return same value.
HeatmapSurveyViewModel.renderHeatmapOverlay() runs synchronously on MainActor. Large point sets could cause UI jank.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify accessibility identifier convention: always use 3-part format {screen}_{element}_{descriptor}. Examples of valid IDs: 'heatmap_button_newProject', 'heatmap_section_emptyState', 'heatmap_list_projects'. The 2-part shorthand (e.g., 'heatmap_emptyState') should not be used even when the element type seems obvious.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Features ios-heatmap-navigation-dashboard and ios-survey-heatmap-save both mixed 2-part and 3-part accessibility IDs in the same file. HeatmapDashboardView.swift has both 'heatmap_emptyState' (2-part) and 'heatmap_button_newProject' (3-part).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Relax the TDD red-phase requirement in ios-ui-worker skill for multi-file features. When a feature creates 5+ interconnected files (Views, ViewModels, platform services), allow implementation-first followed by comprehensive tests. The strict TDD sequence (write failing test → implement → green) doesn't work when types reference each other across files and can't compile in isolation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: 2 of 3 ios-ui-worker sessions (ios-heatmap-navigation-dashboard, ios-survey-heatmap-save) deviated from TDD Step 2, writing implementation first then tests. Both workers acknowledged the deviation and suggested relaxing the requirement for integration-style work.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Codify the SSH fallback explicitly in ios-ui-worker and macos-ui-worker skills. Add: 'Step 6 (testing): Attempt SSH to mac-mini first. If SSH fails or mac-mini git pull fails, fall back to: (1) local `swift test` for NetMonitorCore package tests, (2) local `xcodebuild build` for both targets as build verification.' Currently workers discover the workaround from AGENTS.md known issues, which is fragile.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: 2 of 3 workers (ios-floor-plan-import-calibration, ios-survey-heatmap-save) deviated from the SSH testing step and ran tests locally. Both cited the known pre-existing issue about mac-mini HTTPS authentication being broken.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: avoid deprecated Apple APIs. Specifically, do not use UIScreen.main (deprecated iOS 16) — use GeometryReader or containerSize from parent views instead. This prevents accumulation of deprecation warnings that eventually become errors.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature ios-survey-heatmap-save uses UIScreen.main.bounds in HeatmapSurveyView.swift:353-354 for marker positioning. This deprecated API is unreliable in multi-scene contexts.

rejectedObservations.observation: No convention exists about unique colors for ToolType entries. Worker reused .cyan for wifiHeatmap which is already used by vpnInfo.
rejectedObservations.reason: Insufficient evidence for a new convention. Tool colors are cosmetic and only 2 of 19 tools share a color. A convention would be overly prescriptive for a minor visual detail.

rejectedObservations.observation: WiFiInfoServiceProtocol inconsistent usage — worker calls requestLocationPermission() through protocol in one ViewModel but downcasts in another.
rejectedObservations.reason: Duplicate of the authorizationStatus protocol gap observation. The root cause (missing protocol property) is better addressed as a code task than a convention update.

rejectedObservations.observation: Handoff claims 30 unit tests but actual count is 25. Minor reporting inaccuracy.
rejectedObservations.reason: Cosmetic handoff text issue. Not actionable as a convention or guidance update.

rejectedObservations.observation: RSSI-to-Color mapping logic duplicated in FloatingRSSIHUD and MeasurementDetailSheet.
rejectedObservations.reason: Already tracked as a non-blocking code quality issue. The misc-macos-code-quality feature already covers code deduplication patterns. Not a convention gap.
